title: Pretend Play Coffee Station with Espresso Machine
summary: A kid-sized coffee cart with a working-looking wooden espresso machine — dowel portafilter, steam wand, and knobs. A charming gift build.
description:
This is two builds in one: a little open coffee cart, and the star of the show, a wooden toy espresso machine that sits on top with a dowel portafilter handle, a steam wand, and turned knobs. It's the kind of project that gets played with for years, so it's worth the extra fussing to get the machine's proportions right.

The cart is simple casework — a bottom, two sides, a center panel, and a shelf, all nailed and glued, with the center panel doing the structural work so the nailed joints don't have to be strong. The espresso machine is where the character lives: cut two identical curved sides with a jigsaw, box them together, and add the dowel details. Because little hands will grab the portafilter and wand constantly, glue those dowels well and consider a dab of the right adhesive at the joints.

Round over and sand every edge thoroughly — this is a toy for a small child, so nothing sharp survives, and use a child-safe finish once cured. Cut the dowel pieces slightly long and trim to fit; toy proportions are easier to eyeball than to measure.

tools.note: Cutting the two curved espresso-machine sides.
It's a toy — sand every edge round and smooth.

cutList.note: Cut identical curved profile with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Attach the cart legs and sides
steps.body: Attach the four dowel legs to the bottom shelf with 2" screws, two per leg. Then attach the cart sides to the bottom — nails and glue are fine here because the center panel will reinforce everything.

steps.title: Add the center panel and shelf
steps.body: Attach the center panel with nails and glue — this is the structural spine of the cart. Then set the shelf on top and attach with nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the top
steps.body: Attach the top support piece, then the top itself, with nails and glue. This completes the cart that the espresso machine sits on.

steps.title: Cut the espresso machine body
steps.body: Cut the two espresso-machine sides with a jigsaw — make them identical, and sand the curves smooth. Then box them together with the front and bottom using 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the dowel details
steps.body: Add the drip tray, panel, and top, then the dowel details: the 2" dowel portafilter holder with its 3/4" dowel handle, the two knobs, and the steam wand. Glue these well — small hands grab them constantly. Nail and glue from all sides so nothing works loose.

steps.title: Sand and finish safely
steps.body: Sand every edge and corner round — this is a toy for a small child, so nothing sharp survives. Finish with a child-safe finish and let it fully cure before it goes into play.

images.alt: A children's pretend-play coffee cart with a wooden toy espresso machine
